definitions: Refers to the last person or thing mentioned in a list, but emphasizes that they are equally important or significant as the others.
Used to introduce the final item in a series, indicating that it is not inferior in importance despite its position.
examples: We have our CEO, our CFO, and last but not the least, our dedicated team.
The ingredients include flour, sugar, and last but not the least, a pinch of salt.
The award goes to John, Sarah, and last but not the least, Emily.
